name: Lagunar de Manjavacas – circular by the Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha
name_original: Lagunar de Manjavacas – circular por el Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha
region_intro: Hiking around Mota Del Cuervo offers routes through the unique landscapes of the La Mancha plain, characterized by its extensive flat terrain and significant wetland complexes. The region is known for its elevated areas, providing expansive views, and features a collection of historic windmills on a prominent hill. Rivers like the Záncara also contribute to the natural environment. Best hiking trails around Mota Del Cuervo The most popular hiking route is Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68), a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses varied terrain, offering views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mota del Cuervo – circular by the mills of La Sierra, an easy 2.5 miles (4.1 km) path. This route passes by the iconic windmills, providing panoramic views of the La Mancha plain. Local hikers also love the Lagunar de Manjavacas – circular by the Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha, a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail leading through the significant wetland complex, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Mota Del Cuervo is defined by its extensive wetlands, iconic windmills, and the vast La Mancha plain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Mota Del Cuervo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.3 stars from less than 10 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Mota Del Cuervo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Mota Del Cuervo?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes around Mota Del Cuervo, offering a variety of experiences. These include easy strolls and more moderate excursions through the unique landscapes of the La Mancha plain.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Mota Del Cuervo offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013164664/mota-del-cuervo-circular-by-the-mills-of-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota del Cuervo – circular by the mills of La Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) path that provides panoramic views and is suitable for most fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Many of the trails in Mota Del Cuervo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013172757/santa-maria-de-los-llanos-circular-via-the-cerro-agudo-trail-pr-cu-68" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013169581/lagunar-de-manjavacas-circular-by-the-camino-natural-de-los-humedales-de-la-mancha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunar de Manjavacas – circular by the Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Mota Del Cuervo?
A: Hikers can expect diverse landscapes, from the vast, flat La Mancha plain to elevated areas offering expansive views. The region is also characterized by significant wetland complexes, iconic windmills perched on a hill, and the Záncara River.

Q: Are there any notable attractions or landmarks along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. Many trails pass by significant landmarks. You can explore the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;windmills&lt;/a&gt; of Mota del Cuervo, which offer stunning views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manjavacas Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, a vital wetland, is also a key attraction on some routes, and you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4154343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Hermitage of Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter during a hike?
A: The wetland complexes, particularly around the Manjavacas Lagoon, are crucial for aquatic birdlife. You might spot migratory birds, including whiskered terns, white-headed ducks, and even flamingos, especially during migration seasons. The unique vegetation adapted to high salt concentrations is also a sight to behold.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mota Del Cuervo?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. These seasons are also ideal for birdwatching in the wetlands. Summers can be very hot on the La Mancha plain, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for hiking, especially on clear days.

Q: Are there viewpoints with panoramic views of the region?
A: Yes, Mota del Cuervo is known as the 'Balcón de La Mancha' (Balcony of La Mancha) due to its expansive views. The hill where the historic windmills stand is a prime location for panoramic vistas of the surrounding plain. Many routes incorporate these elevated areas.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in this area?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route and your pace. An easy 2.5-mile route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013164664/mota-del-cuervo-circular-by-the-mills-of-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota del Cuervo – circular by the mills of La Sierra&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 5 minutes. Longer, moderate trails such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013169581/lagunar-de-manjavacas-circular-by-the-camino-natural-de-los-humedales-de-la-mancha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunar de Manjavacas – circular by the Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.5 miles (16.9 km), typically take about 4 hours 18 minutes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mota Del Cuervo?
A: The routes in Mota Del Cuervo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.3 stars. Hikers often praise the unique blend of cultural heritage, like the windmills, and the natural beauty of the wetlands and the vast La Mancha plain.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically explore the famous windmills?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013164664/mota-del-cuervo-circular-by-the-mills-of-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota del Cuervo – circular by the mills of La Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent route that takes you directly past the iconic windmills. This easy path offers a close-up view of these historic structures and panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural areas in Spain are generally dog-friendly, especially on marked trails. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to carry waste bags to maintain the cleanliness of the trails.

Q: Are there historical sites to explore while hiking?
A: Beyond the windmills, the region is rich in history. You can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4154343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Hermitage of Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt;, dating back to the 14th or 15th century, located on a rocky promontory. The area is also part of the renowned Don Quijote Route, connecting you to the landscapes that inspired Cervantes' masterpiece.
tips: The Manjavacas Lagoon Complex Nature Reserve is formed by 4 lagoons: Manjavacas, Sánchez Gómez and La Dehesilla in the municipal term of Mota del Cuervo (Cuenca) and the lagoon of Alcahozo in Pedro Muñoz (Ciudad Real).
Nice and quiet place especially at sunset where the landscape offers very picturesque views.
